facts.text: Goshen’s historic track, opened in 1838, is the oldest half‑mile horse racing track still in use in the United States.
The village’s name comes from the Biblical Land of Goshen, reflecting early settlers’ hopes for fertile soil.
Between Chicago and Jersey City, Erie Railroad’s Erie Limited and Lake Cities stopped at Goshen’s station through the 1950s.
Goshen Public Library’s Local History Room, on the second floor of 366 Main Street, offers digitized newspapers and a microfilm machine for the Goshen Independent Republican.
The original two‑room brick academy building now houses Goshen Town Hall, a landmark noted by a historical marker in the village center.
Goshen’s downtown boasts a blend of historic architecture and modern shops, making it a hub for local culture and community life.
